text.title: AI-Agent Perimeter Policy Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every application of `AI-Agent Perimeter Policy` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Policy MUST be derived from a specific NDA + MSA pair (not from memory). Source clauses are quoted with section refs.
rule.rationale: Memory-derived policies miss clauses; quoting forces re-read.

rule.statement: Policy MUST enumerate allowed tools (Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot, aider, etc.) and BLOCKED tools. 'Default allow' is rejected.
rule.rationale: Allow-list > deny-list when threat surface is uncountable.

rule.statement: Policy MUST enumerate MCP servers in scope + each one's data-handling default. Globally-configured MCP servers count.
rule.rationale: Globally-configured MCP servers leak client code by default.

rule.statement: Policy MUST list per-tool telemetry / log / trace switches set to off (or to an approved sink).
rule.rationale: Defaults send code to provider logs; opt-out per tool.

rule.statement: Policy MUST be re-audited weekly; drift findings logged and remediated within 5 working days.
rule.rationale: Without weekly audit, env-var drift turns NDA into theatre.

rule.statement: Policy MUST declare a breach protocol — incident severity, client notification SLA, rollback plan.
rule.rationale: Without a pre-agreed protocol, breach handling is improvised under pressure.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
